codes.description: An authoritative object exposes a getter or setter instead of a plain data field.
codes.remediation: Replace accessor-backed fields with ordinary enumerable data properties before evaluation.

codes.description: Authoritative kernel input contains a cyclic object or array reference.
codes.remediation: Serialize the input as an acyclic JSON-compatible value graph.

codes.description: Two authoritative records declare the same stable identity in one validation scope.
codes.remediation: Assign unique stable identities and update every affected reference consistently.

codes.description: A strict JSON authority document contains the same object key more than once.
codes.remediation: Remove the duplicate key and supply one unambiguous value.

codes.description: Two selected authority paths resolve to the same underlying file or a selected file changes identity during validation.
codes.remediation: Supply distinct regular files under the trusted root and retry from a stable filesystem state.

codes.description: Kernel input contains a field that could introduce an unauthorized authority source.
codes.remediation: Remove the forbidden field and use only the accepted deterministic authority path.

codes.description: An authority document does not match the SHA-256 bound by Trusted Configuration.
codes.remediation: Restore the exact authorized bytes or issue a new trusted configuration with the correct digest.

codes.description: The trusted-configuration loader received a missing or invalid invocation argument.
codes.remediation: Provide non-empty trusted configuration and repository-root paths.

codes.description: An authoritative array is malformed, sparse, extended, or does not use the ordinary Array prototype.
codes.remediation: Use a dense ordinary array containing only accepted JSON-compatible values.

codes.description: Evidence Contract selection provenance is outside the accepted trusted configuration or deterministic Policy paths.
codes.remediation: Supply selection provenance from an authorized Trusted Configuration or deterministic Policy selection.

codes.description: A field requiring a JSON primitive received an object, array, undefined value, or unsupported number.
codes.remediation: Use null, a boolean, a string, or a finite JSON number as required.

codes.description: An authoritative field does not equal the exact protocol literal required at that path.
codes.remediation: Replace the value with the documented exact literal for the selected schema version.

codes.description: A Policy-owned Rule denial code is not a stable uppercase machine token.
codes.remediation: Use a non-empty code matching the accepted uppercase token format.

codes.description: An authoritative record references a missing or inconsistent related identity.
codes.remediation: Repair the referenced identity and its bidirectional membership without fabricating Evidence.

codes.description: A Rule declares authority outside Trusted Configuration or Policy provenance.
codes.remediation: Bind the Rule to an authorized Trusted Configuration or versioned Policy.

codes.description: A Rule predicate is outside the deterministic predicate set supported by the current kernel.
codes.remediation: Use an accepted predicate or select a separately authorized kernel version.

codes.description: An identity is empty or contains characters outside the stable identity alphabet.
codes.remediation: Provide a non-empty identity using only letters, digits, period, underscore, or hyphen.

codes.description: A required authoritative string is missing or empty.
codes.remediation: Provide a non-empty string with the meaning required at the reported path.

codes.description: A selected product input file is not valid UTF-8.
codes.remediation: Re-encode the complete file as valid UTF-8 without changing its authorized meaning.

codes.description: An applicable Evidence Requirement has no acceptable Evidence from the supplied authorized inputs.
codes.remediation: Provide an authorized matching Observation or Verification Receipt and run a new evaluation.

codes.description: A strict authority document is not valid unambiguous JSON.
codes.remediation: Correct the JSON syntax and remove duplicate or trailing content before reauthorization.

codes.description: An authoritative kernel record omits a required field.
codes.remediation: Add the required field with a valid value from the authorized source.

codes.description: An authoritative object or array contains a hidden non-enumerable field.
codes.remediation: Rebuild the input from ordinary enumerable JSON data properties.

codes.description: Authoritative input contains a value that JSON cannot represent deterministically.
codes.remediation: Replace the value with an accepted JSON-compatible value.

codes.description: An authoritative record uses a class instance or non-plain object prototype.
codes.remediation: Convert the record to an ordinary plain object before evaluation.

codes.description: A selected input path is not a regular file.
codes.remediation: Select a regular non-symbolic file within the allowed root.

codes.description: A selected authority path is absolute, escapes the trusted root, or violates the accepted repository-path form.
codes.remediation: Use a safe relative repository path contained by the trusted root.

codes.description: The bounded release delivery workflow failed at the separately reported stage.
codes.remediation: Inspect the machine-readable stage, correct that boundary condition, and retry without changing authority.

codes.description: Kernel input contains a Proxy-backed value whose behavior cannot be inspected deterministically.
codes.remediation: Materialize the value as an ordinary plain object or array before evaluation.

codes.description: A required local file could not be opened, inspected, or read completely.
codes.remediation: Restore stable read access to the selected regular file and retry.

codes.description: Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, observer, or target references do not agree.
codes.remediation: Supply a mutually consistent authorized document set with exact identities and versions.

codes.description: A Policy-owned Rule attempts to emit a reason code reserved for kernel-owned classification.
codes.remediation: Choose a distinct Policy-owned stable reason code.

codes.description: A product Rule attempts to use the Foundation HARN_ governance namespace.
codes.remediation: Use a Policy-owned product code that does not begin with HARN_.

codes.description: The supplied repository root resolves through an alias rather than its canonical path.
codes.remediation: Invoke the loader with the canonical repository-root path.

codes.description: The supplied repository root is missing, unreadable, symbolic, or not a directory.
codes.remediation: Provide an accessible canonical directory as the trusted repository root.

codes.description: An output path aliases an input or another protected file.
codes.remediation: Choose a distinct output file that does not share filesystem identity with protected inputs.

codes.description: Trusted Configuration, Policy, or Evidence Contract content violates its accepted closed shape.
codes.remediation: Correct the authority document to the supported schema and reestablish its exact digest binding.

codes.description: The supplied GitHub pull-request snapshot is malformed, ambiguous, incomplete, or internally duplicated.
codes.remediation: Collect a fresh bounded snapshot that satisfies the exact closed snapshot contract.

codes.description: Authoritative input contains a symbol-keyed property that JSON cannot represent.
codes.remediation: Remove symbol keys and use only ordinary string-keyed JSON fields.

codes.description: The observed repository, pull request, base, or head identity differs from Trusted Configuration.
codes.remediation: Collect the exact configured target or issue new Trusted Configuration for the intended target.

codes.description: Claims, Evidence Contracts, Evidence Requirements, or Observations do not share the declared evaluation scope.
codes.remediation: Align all authoritative records to the exact selected target scope.

codes.description: A bounded product input exceeds the maximum accepted byte length.
codes.remediation: Reduce the input within the documented limit without truncating authority-bearing content.

codes.description: An authoritative record contains a field outside its closed protocol shape.
codes.remediation: Remove the unknown field or use a separately authorized schema version that defines it.

codes.description: A caller attempted to use a configuration object that did not pass the trusted loader boundary.
codes.remediation: Load and validate the exact authority documents through the trusted configuration loader.

codes.description: A staged local output could not be created, finalized, or cleaned up safely.
codes.remediation: Restore stable write and rename access to the output directory, inspect any reported orphan, and retry.
